experience.shortDescription: Managed a talented team of Queen's students to ideate, develop, and market a software product
bullets: Led a cross-functional team of developers and business analysts to ideate, build, and ship a software product from the ground up
Defined product roadmap and priorities, balancing technical feasibility with user needs and stakeholder expectations
Presented product pitches and progress updates to students, faculty, and potential investors throughout the academic year

experience.shortDescription: Developed the website and other software for Queen's University's official MLH hackathon
bullets: Built and deployed the official QHacks 2020 website using React and Gatsby, attracting over 10,000 unique visitors during the application cycle
Developed the applicant dashboard used by 700+ hackers in the weeks leading up to and during the event
Coordinated all technology operations on the day of the hackathon, supporting teams and keeping event information current in real time

experience.shortDescription: Created a network of in-person and online quality tech help and tutoring around the GTA
bullets: Co-founded technology help company, providing tech tutoring and support to 20+ customers in the first 3 months of operation across Toronto
Overseeing all technical aspects of operations, including web development, database management, and session scheduling
Company was able to turn a profit in first month of operations

projects.name: PupBot
projects.technologies: Python, Matplotlib, Selenium, Twitter API, Twilio API, Heroku
projects.shortDescription: A Twitter and Twilio bot tweeting hourly price updates of an ETH-based altcoin
bullets: A bot developed from scratch to fetch and post hourly price "pupdates" for Puppy Coin, a community-driven, Ethereum-based altcoin
Selenium and CryptoCompare API fetch Puppy Coin and Ethereum prices
On the hour, Twitter and Twilio APIs tweet updates and send position values to specified Twitter users and phone numbers
Deployed to Heroku and scheduled to run each hour

projects.name: TicTacToe
projects.shortDescription: A basic, text-based, Pythonic version of tic-tac-toe made in under an hour
bullets: An extremely basic, text-based version of tic-tac-toe
One of my earliest coding projects, developed in about an hour on a flight without access to any online resources
Initially written in Python 2 and ported to Python 3
